text:
In a quiet forest clearing, an owl sat on a branch high above a babbling brook.

Once every silver moon, an apprentice woodworker arrived seeking rare crafting materials. This time it was a young woman named Laurel.

The owl hooted softly, recognizing the hum of her thoughts. "I've been searching for moons' silver," she whispered.

The owl inclined its head, offering guidance. Laurel journeyed deeper into the woods. Her footsteps quiet against leaf-covered paths.

Midway through an ancient oak tree, she found it. Silver-like droplets collected on moss, twinkling like stars.

Unsure of what they could do with such an odd treasure, Laurel tucked the dewdrops into her pack.

Days passed, and Laurel crafted an intricate vessel, its surface reflecting dewdrops endlessly.

Back at her little cabin, she illuminated the vessel with a campfire. Streams of light pranced in time to an unheard melody Laurel hummed.

The villagers marveled. Laurel was no artisan
